fields.description:
ABOUT THE TEAM

The AI Deployment Management (ADM) team enables organizations to turn OpenAI products into real, sustained impact through world-class enablement and training execution.

Our mission is to help customers successfully adopt and operationalize AI across their organizations. We partner with enterprises to translate the potential of OpenAI’s technology into durable capability—through structured training, technical enablement, and scalable deployment programs.

By helping customers move from experimentation to production, the ADM team accelerates time-to-value, deepens product adoption, and helps make OpenAI indispensable to how organizations work.


ABOUT THE ROLE

We are looking for a Lead, Builder ADM to lead a team responsible for enabling enterprise customers to successfully deploy and adopt OpenAI products in the Enterprise.

This role combines technical leadership, customer enablement, and people management. You will build and develop a team of AI Deployment Managers while shaping scalable enablement programs that help organizations operationalize frontier AI technologies across our existing and emerging product suite.

You will work closely with Sales, Technical Success, and Product teams to ensure customers are equipped with the knowledge, tools, and best practices needed to successfully deploy OpenAI products across their organizations.


IN THIS ROLE YOU WILL


LEAD CUSTOMER ENABLEMENT

 - Own the technical enablement strategy for OpenAI’s products, including ChatGPT Enterprise, Codex, Agents, and APIs
   

 - Lead customer training and services across the full lifecycle—from onboarding to expansion and long-term adoption
   

 - Design and deliver high-impact engagements including enterprise onboarding, developer workshops, executive briefings, and technical bootcamps
   

 - Drive customer activation, sustained usage, and measurable business value through structured enablement programs
   

 - Develop scalable enablement assets, playbooks, and best practices based on patterns observed across customers


BUILD AND LEAD A HIGH-PERFORMING TEAM

 - Hire, onboard, and develop a team of Technical AI Deployment Managers
   

 - Provide mentorship and coaching to team members delivering enterprise enablement programs
   

 - Establish best practices for technical enablement delivery across regions and customer segments
   

 - Set clear goals and performance metrics aligned with customer adoption and product success


DRIVE OPERATIONAL AND STRATEGIC IMPACT

 - Own deployment strategy for ADM engagements across enterprise customers
   

 - Identify patterns across customer deployments and translate insights into scalable enablement frameworks
   

 - Partner with product and go-to-market teams to incorporate customer insights into roadmap and strategy
   

 - Improve enablement tooling, operational processes, and program scalability
   

Managers in this role may also lead strategic enterprise enablement engagements, including executive briefings and advanced technical workshops.


GROWTH PATH

This role is begins as a Lead, Technical ADM with a mandate to establish the global function and manage the first ~5 ICs hired. As the model proves impactful, larger teams, additional regions, and broad ownership of OpenAI’s developer adoption programs may be included in the scope. Success in this role positions the lead to influence how OAI scales expertise globally.


YOU MIGHT THRIVE IN THIS ROLE IF YOU

 - Have experience leading technical, customer-facing teams such as solutions engineering, technical success, developer relations, or technical enablement
   

 - Are comfortable explaining complex technical systems to both executive and developer audiences
   

 - Have strong technical depth across APIs, coding, agents, and AI systems
   

 - Understand how AI systems are deployed in production, including concepts like RAG, model evaluation, fine-tuning, and system tradeoffs
   

 - Have led technical trainings, workshops, or enablement programs for enterprise customers
   

 - Can connect technical capabilities to real business outcomes such as productivity, efficiency, and revenue impact
   

 - Thrive in fast-moving environments and enjoy learning new technologies quickly
   


WHY THIS ROLE

Lead a frontier AI enablement team
You will help define how OpenAI enables enterprise organizations to adopt AI at scale.

Scale impact through people
Rather than supporting a handful of customers yourself, you will develop technical leaders who enable hundreds of organizations adopting AI.

Shape OpenAI’s enterprise playbook
Your team will influence how enterprises operationalize frontier AI technologies, shaping product feedback loops and adoption strategies.

Turn cutting-edge AI into real-world impact
This role sits where frontier AI meets real-world deployment—helping organizations move from experimentation to durable production use.

About OpenAI

OpenAI is an AI research and deployment company dedicated to ensuring that general-purpose artificial intelligence benefits all of humanity. We push the boundaries of the capabilities of AI systems and seek to safely deploy them to the world through our products. AI is an extremely powerful tool that must be created with safety and human needs at its core, and to achieve our mission, we must encompass and value the many different perspectives, voices, and experiences that form the full spectrum of humanity. 

We are an equal opportunity employer, and we do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, age, veteran status, disability, genetic information, or other applicable legally protected characteristic.

For additional information, please see OpenAI’s Affirmative Action and Equal Employment Opportunity Policy Statement https://cdn.openai.com/policies/eeo-policy-statement.pdf.

Background checks for applicants will be administered in accordance with applicable law, and qualified applicants with arrest or conviction records will be considered for employment consistent with those laws, including the San Francisco Fair Chance Ordinance, the Los Angeles County Fair Chance Ordinance for Employers, and the California Fair Chance Act, for US-based candidates. For unincorporated Los Angeles County workers: we reasonably believe that criminal history may have a direct, adverse and negative relationship with the following job duties, potentially resulting in the withdrawal of a conditional offer of employment: protect computer hardware entrusted to you from theft, loss or damage; return all computer hardware in your possession (including the data contained therein) upon termination of employment or end of assignment; and maintain the confidentiality of proprietary, confidential, and non-public information. In addition, job duties require access to secure and protected information technology systems and related data security obligations.

To notify OpenAI that you believe this job posting is non-compliant, please submit a report through this form https://form.asana.com/?d=57018692298241&k=5MqR40fZd7jlxVUh5J-UeA. No response will be provided to inquiries unrelated to job posting compliance.

We are committed to providing reasonable accommodations to applicants with disabilities, and requests can be made via this link https://form.asana.com/?k=bQ7w9h3iexRlicUdWRiwvg&d=57018692298241.

OpenAI Global Applicant Privacy Policy https://cdn.openai.com/policies/global-employee-and-contractor-privacy-policy.pdf

At OpenAI, we believe artificial intelligence has the potential to help people solve immense global challenges, and we want the upside of AI to be widely shared. Join us in shaping the future of technology.
fields.title: Lead, Builder AI Deployment Manager
